BEAR MOUNTAIN AT SUDBURY

CCN: 225710 · SUDBURY, MA 01776 · Middlesex County

Overview

Address
136 BOSTON POST ROAD, SUDBURY, MA 01776
Phone
9784432722
Certified beds
142
Avg daily residents
74 (52% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1996-07-26
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.73 3.71 3.88 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.71 0.65 0.74 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.82 0.82 0.86 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.20 2.24 2.28 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.54 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.50
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.41
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.64
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
38.2%
annual
RN turnover
62.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.31
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.95
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-05 10 10 0 36 1 36
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-11-02 26 20 6 116 1 116
Total weighted health score
56.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (48)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
43 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-01-15 2025-02-18
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. B 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-11-05 2024-12-19
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2024-06-18 2024-07-26
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-06-18 2024-07-26
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. B 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. B 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. B 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. G 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-18
0943 Give their staff education on dementia care, and what abuse, neglect, and exploitation are; and how to report abuse, neglect, and exploitation. D 2023-08-09 2023-08-31
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2023-06-13 2023-07-17
0675 Honor each resident's preferences, choices, values and beliefs. E 2023-06-13 2023-07-17
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. E 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-04-14 2022-05-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2022-04-14 2022-05-20

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
